final_test_output:
1	#!/bin/sh
     2	# Test for rate limit bypass via X-Forwarded-For header rotation.
     3	# The rate limiter should limit requests per actual client, not per
     4	# spoofed header value.
     5	
     6	set -e
     7	
     8	FAIL=0
     9	PORT=18084
    10	
    11	# Rebuild TypeScript
    12	cd /app/app && npx tsc 2>&1
    13	cd /app
    14	
    15	# Start server
    16	PORT=$PORT node /app/app/dist/server.js &
    17	SERVER_PID=$!
    18	sleep 1
    19	
    20	if ! kill -0 $SERVER_PID 2>/dev/null; then
    21	    echo "FAIL: Server failed to start"
    22	    exit 1
    23	fi
    24	
    25	# Test 1: Verify rate limiter works for normal requests (no spoofed header)
    26	echo "Sending 15 requests without header spoofing (limit is 10)..."
    27	PASSED=0
    28	BLOCKED=0
    29	for i in $(seq 1 15); do
    30	    STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:$PORT/api/users)
    31	    if [ "$STATUS" = "200" ]; then
    32	        PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
    33	    elif [ "$STATUS" = "429" ]; then
    34	        BLOCKED=$((BLOCKED + 1))
    35	    fi
    36	done
    37	echo "Without spoofing: $PASSED passed, $BLOCKED blocked"
    38	
    39	if [ "$BLOCKED" -lt 1 ]; then
    40	    echo "FAIL: Rate limiter did not block any of 15 requests (limit is 10)"
    41	    FAIL=1
    42	else
    43	    echo "PASS: Rate limiter correctly blocked excess requests"
    44	fi
    45	
    46	# Wait for rate limit window to reset (window is 5 seconds)
    47	sleep 6
    48	
    49	# Test 2: Attempt bypass — rotate X-Forwarded-For header per request
    50	echo "Sending 30 requests with rotating X-Forwarded-For header (limit is 10)..."
    51	BYPASS_PASSED=0
    52	BYPASS_BLOCKED=0
    53	for i in $(seq 1 30); do
    54	    STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' \
    55	        -H "X-Forwarded-For: 10.0.0.$i" \
    56	        http://localhost:$PORT/api/users)
    57	    if [ "$STATUS" = "200" ]; then
    58	        BYPASS_PASSED=$((BYPASS_PASSED +
